title: Exclude features derived from the target
description: Audit each feature for hidden dependence on the label so the model does not learn a proxy for the answer it should predict.
body: Use when adding features that are computed near or after the outcome being predicted. Avoid features built from post-outcome aggregates, IDs that encode the label, or fields populated only once the event resolved — they leak the target. Actions: trace each feature's data source and timing, drop any computed from or after the label, and verify by checking that removing a suspiciously strong feature does not collapse a sane baseline. Validation signals include a feature provenance audit and a near-perfect single-feature signal flagged for review. Risk boundary: target leakage produces models that fail completely in production; if a feature's timing relative to the label is unclear, deny its use and escalate for review.
